name: TimescaleDB
description: PostgreSQL extension for high-performance time-series analytics with automatic partitioning

website.comment: Timescale Inc. rebranded to TigerData in June 2025

license.comment: Dual-licensed: core features under Apache 2.0, advanced features under Timescale License (TSL). TSL is source-available but not OSI-approved

hosting-options.comment: Tiger Cloud (official managed), self-hosted via extension, available on Supabase and other providers

initial-release.comment: Open-sourced in April 2017; first stable release (1.0) in October 2018

data-model.comment: PostgreSQL extension optimized for time-series data, inherits relational model

topology.comment: Uses PostgreSQL streaming replication (leader-follower). Multi-node distributed hypertables deprecated in v2.14

sharding.comment: Automatic time-based partitioning via hypertables (chunks). Multi-node sharding deprecated

replication-factor.comment: Inherits PostgreSQL replication; Tiger Cloud offers HA with read replicas

multi-region.comment: Multi-node distributed hypertables deprecated in v2.14. No native multi-region support currently

cap-position.comment: Inherits PostgreSQL's CP characteristics with strong consistency

consistency-model.comment: Inherits PostgreSQL ACID compliance with full consistency guarantees

acid-transactions.comment: Full ACID compliance inherited from PostgreSQL

transaction-scope.comment: Single-node: full PostgreSQL transaction scope. Multi-node was deprecated

distributed-transactions.comment: Multi-node support deprecated in v2.14; single-node only now

query-language.comment: Full PostgreSQL SQL plus time-series-specific functions (time_bucket, continuous aggregates)

sql-compatibility.comment: Native PostgreSQL extension with full SQL and wire protocol compatibility

secondary-indexes.comment: Full PostgreSQL index support (B-tree, hash, GiST, GIN, BRIN)

joins.comment: Full PostgreSQL join support across tables and hypertables

aggregations.comment: Full SQL aggregations plus continuous aggregates for pre-computed time-series analytics

max-cluster-size.comment: Single-node focused after multi-node deprecation. Vendor reports scaling to 2M inserts/sec on single node

write-throughput.comment: TSBS benchmark: peaks at 1.2M rows/sec for 1K hosts. Cloudflare production: ~100K aggregated rows/sec

read-latency-p99.comment: Cloudflare reports query latencies in hundreds of ms for most customers; continuous aggregates improve this significantly

auto-scaling.comment: Tiger Cloud: automatic storage scaling. Compute requires manual adjustment

hot-spot-handling.comment: Time-based chunking naturally distributes time-series writes across partitions

zero-downtime-upgrades.comment: Tiger Cloud offers rolling upgrades; self-hosted follows PostgreSQL upgrade patterns

point-in-time-recovery.comment: Tiger Cloud includes PITR; self-hosted uses PostgreSQL WAL archiving

backup-strategy.comment: Supports PostgreSQL backup methods including pg_dump, pg_basebackup, and continuous WAL archiving

kubernetes-operator.comment: Official Helm charts available but no dedicated operator. Compatible with CloudNativePG operator

open-source.comment: Core features are Apache 2.0, but advanced features (compression, continuous aggregates) require TSL which is source-available, not OSI-approved

free-tier.comment: Tiger Cloud Free plan: up to 2 services, 750MB storage per service, shared compute

pricing-model.comment: Hourly compute billing + per-GB storage. Performance plan starts at $30/mo compute + $0.177/GB-mo storage

cost-estimate-small.comment: Estimate based on Tiger Cloud Performance/Scale plans. Single-node architecture after multi-node deprecation

change-data-capture.comment: Via PostgreSQL logical replication. Debezium supports TimescaleDB. Also timescaledb-event-streamer for hypertable CDC

watch-subscribe.comment: No native push-based subscription mechanism; requires polling or external CDC tools

event-streaming.comment: Via Debezium Kafka connector with TimescaleDB transformation. Also supports timescaledb-event-streamer for Kafka/NATS
